current_focus: Track a Pareto frontier across reconstruction quality and sparsity/cost. Treat K=128 as one anchor point, not the only success criterion. Prefer experiments that add non-dominated tradeoff points, including smaller-K runs that accept some FVU increase when they improve the overall frontier.

topk.design_hypothesis: With topk K=128 EF=12 now showing a better anchor at signum LR=1e-3, switching only the optimizer to adam can test whether the remaining gap is optimization-limited rather than learning-rate-limited.
next_steps: If adam improves the curve or final FVU, keep adam fixed and do one LR callback for adam next.
If adam underperforms signum at the same LR, retain signum as mainline and only revisit optimizer later if curve shape suggests a specific failure mode.

recent_rounds.hypothesis: The reference topk recipe at K=128, EF=12, signum, LR=8e-4 should provide a clean first quality-anchor curve and runtime baseline; that anchor is needed before any LR or optimizer retuning can be interpreted.
A modest LR reduction from 8e-4 to 6e-4 may improve the topk K=128 EF=12 signum training trajectory by reducing late-stage noise or overshoot while preserving the current architecture and optimizer.
The baseline topk K=128 EF=12 signum recipe may be slightly underpowered at LR=8e-4; increasing LR to 1e-3 can test whether the worse 6e-4 result came from under-training rather than instability.
With topk K=128 EF=12 now showing a better anchor at signum LR=1e-3, switching only the optimizer to adam can test whether the remaining gap is optimization-limited rather than learning-rate-limited.

recent_insights: NOTE: Use this run as the clean topk curve and runtime anchor before any LR or optimizer comparison.
